channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Anti-Immigrant Marches Held Across South Africa
item.description: Demonstrators had given undocumented migrants until Tuesday to self-deport or face consequences.

item.title: Small-Plane Crash Tests China’s Security State
item.description: How did a private aircraft enter Beijing’s sensitive airspace?

item.title: Washington’s Cuba Policy Is Self-Sabotage
item.description: The U.S. blockade is destroying Havana’s chances of becoming stable and democratic.

item.title: Allies learn how to bully America
item.description: Can Taiwan use its chipmaking industry to strong-arm Donald Trump?

item.title: A new Plaza Accord for global currencies wouldn’t work
item.description: The conditions and will for a deal are not what they were in 1985

item.title: Doomsday Climate Scenarios Were Wrong. That Doesn’t Help Europe.
item.description: Recent scientific reassessments strengthen the case for rapid climate action rather than weaken it.

item.title: Meeting Jordan Bardella, France’s possible president
item.description: The 30-year-old populist will run if Marine Le Pen is banned. Who is he?

item.title: The European Country That Figured Out Trump’s White House
item.description: Poland has been successful partly due to strong personal ties, but history suggests that its luck may not hold forever.

item.title: Israel election poll tracker: will Binyamin Netanyahu hold on to power?
item.description: The Economist is tracking the contest for Israel’s Knesset

item.title: Moment of Truth: The Case Against Orda.kz Editor Begins
item.description: The case against former Orda editor-in-chief Gulnara Bazhkenova, who was placed under house arrest in December 2025, is set to begin following months of legal proceedings.

item.title: How Modi Has Remade India
item.description: Over the last 12 years, Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has overseen dizzying progress in economic modernization and state capacity-building, as well as dismaying setbacks on institutional independence and minority inclusion. This record offers important insights into how India will navigate the 21st century.

item.title: Israel Has a Hezbollah Drone Problem
item.description: “We don’t have a solution,” an Israeli military official said.

item.title: Donald Trump’s AI regime is opaque, unpredictable—and unsustainable
item.description: The administration now controls who gets access to the best models

item.title: Water Makes the World Go Round
item.description: Despite being vital to health, security, and economic prosperity, water is often taken for granted. As supplies come under growing strain, water-related considerations will shape economic policy and political decision-making, as well as firms’ investment decisions, risk assessments, and corporate strategies.

item.title: China’s $295 Billion Ambitions for AI Will Drive up Domestic High-Tech Stocks
item.description: Beijing’s AI data center strategy will purposefully exclude foreign firms – providing a shot in the arm for domestic companies.

item.title: Donald Trump’s hope for a new Middle East is premature
item.description: Recent attacks underscore the limits of diplomacy between Iran and America

item.title: China’s ‘Ethnic Unity’ Cup: Football and Assimilation in the Uyghur Region
item.description: A football tournament is China’s latest propaganda campaign to whitewash its abuses of Uyghur people – and deconstruct Uyghur identity in favor of Chinese “unity.”

item.title: The Industrial Gap in India-Japan Economic Security
item.description: India and Japan keep talking about diversifying supply chains, but there's a gap between diplomatic rhetoric and industrial reality.

item.title: Xi Jinping Just Made It a Crime to Be Taiwanese
item.description: China’s attack on Taiwan has already started – with the Ethnic Unity Law as a weapon.

item.title: The Real Competitiveness Trap
item.description: Policymakers obsessed with competitiveness both in Europe and beyond need to abandon old orthodoxies. New research finds that for two decades, capital in Europe has been abundant and cheap, and corporate profits strong, yet investment, productivity, and wages have stalled, and labor's share of total income has shrunk.

item.title: Britain’s New ‘King’ Will Decide Its Fate
item.description: Andy Burnham is on the verge of taking power—and of being confronted with his own limitations.

item.title: For Israel, the Oct. 7 War Is Effectively Over
item.description: In 984 days of fighting, “total victory” eluded Netanyahu on all fronts.

item.title: Iranian Elites Are Not in Agreement About What to Do Next
item.description: If the United States wants the cease-fire to last, it needs to take Tehran’s politics seriously.

item.title: Trump Isn’t Actually Abandoning the U.N. System
item.description: Even this U.S. administration is confronting limits to the politics of exit.

item.title: Is Washington Endorsing Family Rule in Libya?
item.description: The United States should heed its own track record of backing individuals in conflict-affected states.

item.title: Analysing Africa newsletter: South Africa is in denial about xenophobia
item.description: John McDermott, our chief Africa correspondent, analyses the country’s shocking rise in anti-foreign sentiment

item.title: Australia, Vanuatu Sign Security Pact
item.description: The Nakamal Agreement between Australia and Vanuatu may be weaker than Canberra’s other regional pacts, but it remains significant in checking China’s influence.

item.title: The War Room newsletter: The country with no army
item.description: A transatlantic rift is unsettling the land of fire and ice, writes Anton La Guardia, our diplomatic editor

item.title: How to Empower Africa’s Multilateral Development Banks
item.description: Capitalizing multilateral development banks in Africa is a strategic investment in the continent’s economic transformation. To mobilize adequate funding will require leveraging domestic savings, pursuing monetary integration, using innovative financing mechanisms, and strengthening regional capital markets.

item.title: Japan-Philippines Comprehensive Strategic Partnership
item.description: Will the rules-based order be a beacon in troubled waters?

item.title: El Boletín newsletter: An earthquake shakes Venezuela’s political reset
item.description: Our correspondent in Caracas reports on how disaster is testing an already fragile country

item.title: Sri Lanka’s Easter Blasts Probe Enters the Intelligence Architecture
item.description: While not every arrest will lead to a conviction, the old assumption that power could delay accountability indefinitely has begun to shake.

item.title: Extreme Weather Will Upend U.S.-China Competition
item.description: The cost of falling behind on climate adaptation.

item.title: In Photos: Northeast Thailand’s Rocket Festival
item.description: The annual bung bang fai festival calls on the god Phaya Thaen to bring rain at the start of the agricultural season.

item.title: Arrests Spike as Vietnam Sees New ‘Wave of Repression,’ Rights Group Says
item.description: The Bangkok-based advocacy group Project88 documented 56 political arrests last year, the most in any year since 2018.

item.title: How the Kem Ley Assassination Changed Cambodian Politics
item.description: A decade on from one of the country's highest-profile political killings, democracy remains a dream rather than reality.

item.title: Malaysia Extends Search for Missing Malaysia Airlines Flight MH370
item.description: The maritime exploration firm Ocean Infinity will have an additional year to locate the wreckage of the Boeing 777 airliner, which vanished without trace in 2014.

item.title: The Supreme Court has handed Donald Trump yet more power
item.description: Dozens of agencies—but not the Federal Reserve—are now subject to presidential whim

item.title: Southeast Asia’s Toxic Rivers: No Easy Solutions
item.description: Scientists warn of a regional disaster If pollution from rare earth and gold mining in Myanmar continues to spread toxins further along the Mekong River.

item.title: A Mid-Year Check on US Asia Policy
item.description: Goodbye INDOPACOM and hello (again) Middle Eastern distractions.
